channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abondened vehicle, 11th April
item.description:
This vehicle has been parked next to hour house for more than a month and it has not moved at all.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Raeburn Road, Falconwood, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: No parking faded, 11th April
item.description:
The no parking on the chicane has faded and the yellow line added as part of the MacDonald Road project covers only 1/3rd so people now think they can park there. Either the white lines need to be refreshed or the double yellow lines extended.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Friern Barnet Road, Friern Barnet, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flypostering, 11th April
item.description:
Old Kent Road Remove flypostering from bus shelter

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 840, Old Kent Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Formal Complaint: Ongoing Waste Nuisance and Environmental Health Concern, 11th April
item.description:
I am reporting an ongoing waste issue caused by neighbouring properties on my road in Erith. Their bins are consistently overflowing, and food waste (including items such as rice) is regularly left scattered across the street. This is not a one-off , it happens repeatedly and is getting worse. The rubbish does not remain contained to their property. It spreads onto the public road and frequently ends up directly outside my home, including on my front porch. This is extremely unhygienic and unacceptable. This situation presents a clear environmental health risk, including the potential to attract vermin and create unsanitary living conditions. It is also significantly impacting the cleanliness and standard of the neighbourhood. This is not simply a street cleaning issue (which rarely ever happens anyway, the street is always so filthy, it is actually starting to feel regretful that we bought a family home here). The root cause is the ongoing failure to manage waste properly at the source. Unless this is addressed, the problem will continue. I request that this is urgently investigated by Environmental Health / Waste Enforcement and that appropriate enforcement action is taken against the responsible property. I am able to provide photographic evidence if required.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Johns Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-post to bus shelter re, 11th April
item.description:
Man with van

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brixton Road, Oval, Greater London

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Light out - roundabout, 11th April
item.description:
Street light in the middle.of thr roundabout is out/not working
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - damage to fence & Rats now present, 11th April
item.description:
This alleyway next to 29/30 Woodchurch Close DA14 6QH is constantly being used for people to dump rubbish, I know who is dumping the rubbish as it’s the same family and they have now caused damage to my partners fence and today Saturday 11th April I discovered RATS when looking at the damage to the fence. This has been ongoing for a while but has since ramped up with a lot more rubbish, including branches from trees they cut down. I witness the dumping both on Sunday 5th April & today. I have a video and further photos if required. I am concerned with the rats and the frequency of the dumping and now the damage to my partners property.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29,30, Woodchurch Close, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 11th April
item.description:
Items dumped again in car parking area - garden furniture and household furniture
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fieldfare Road, Greater London

item.title: Discarded canisters, 11th April
item.description:

item.title: Overgrown shrubs, 11th April
item.description:
I believe this is council land. It is never attended to by the contractors. I have been dealing with it. The house at number one is unattended. Please could this be looked into. The overgrown shrubs limit parking.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Baytree Close, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large hole appearing in road near Armstrong Close, 11th April
item.description:
Large hole appearing, approximately 6 inches deep. Buses making loud noise when driving over, will become very dangerous if it gets worse.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southlands Grove, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
